steps.title: Identify the temporal context
steps.content: The passage establishes a completed historical period (1912–1951) using past tense verbs (“owned,” “operated,” “was”), anchoring all described actions in the past.

steps.title: Apply tense consistency
steps.content:
The rule is tense consistency (verbs in the same temporal context must share the same tense), and “became” is the only past tense form, making it correct.

The other choices violate this rule—”will become” is future, “is becoming” is present progressive, and “to become” is infinitive form, none aligning with the past context.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
